metadata.name: Onyx
metadata.title: Onyx Settings
metadata.description: When laser cleaning onyx, make sure you watch its banded layers closely, unlike smoother marbles that heat evenly—onyx absorbs energy fast because of its dense quartz makeup, so start with lower power to prevent cracking those fine stripes. You must adjust scan speeds slower than for granites, since its poor heat spread traps warmth locally and risks surface checks if you rush. This keeps the natural polish intact for heritage pieces, avoiding dull spots from overexposure.

author.name: Yi-Chun Lin
author.title: Ph.D.
credentials: Ph.D. Materials Engineering, National Taiwan University, 2018
Post-Ph.D. fellowship at TSMC's laser fab lab, 2018-2020
Assisted in projects on ultrafast laser applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Onyx surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Onyx absorption and minimal subsurface damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

dwellTime.description: Optimal dwell time per spot for effective cleaning

power.description: Typical range for Nd:YAG lasers in stone cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for light contamination on Onyx (chalcedony-based, low thermal conductivity); max safe below 500W to prevent cracking (based on IPG Photonics specs and studies in J. Cult. Herit. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal; 500 mm/s recommended for optimal balance on Onyx; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (500-2000 mm/s) for light; from industry manuals like those from Adapt Laser Systems.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for industrial cleaning to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ) on heat-sensitive Onyx; 20 ns recommended over ps/fs for cost-effectiveness while reducing thermal accumulation (laser physics in Opt. Lasers Eng. 2020).

repetition_rate.description: Common for 1064 nm cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for Onyx to balance speed and heat buildup (high freq risks accumulation on low-conductivity substrates); trade-off noted in Appl. Surf. Sci. 2019.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; Onyx damage threshold ~6-8 J/cm² (SiO2-based); 1.5 J/cm² recommended for cleaning without substrate etching (ablation studies in J. Appl. Phys. 2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for uniform coverage; 300 μm recommended for Onyx surface preparation (fine detail vs. large area); from equipment specs like Coherent lasers, suitable for stone texturing.

pass_count.description: Typically 1-3 passes for complete removal on stones; 2 passes recommended for Onyx to ensure uniformity without overexposure; based on case studies in industrial cleaning (Laser Cleaning Handbook, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 50% recommended for Onyx to avoid gaps or excessive overlap-induced damage; typical range from process optimization in Opt. Express 2016.
